conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a child - my little one Alex, who's 3 years old. They live with me and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great that Alex lives with you.

To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: are you or your partner responsible for Alex? This means you're the main person looking after them and paying towards their upbringing (such as for food, clothes, or housing).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely! I'm responsible for Alex. I look after them and pay for everything they need - their food, clothes, housing, all of it.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! 

Can you confirm that Alex is under 16 years old (or under 20 if they're in approved education or training)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is only 3 years old, so definitely under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Now I need to ask about your residency status. Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside here (for example, you're a UK citizen, have settled status, or have the right to work/live in the UK)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Just a couple more questions to make sure I have all the information I need.

Is Alex currently living with you in your household, or are they living with someone else, in hospital, or in care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me in my household.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. One final question: is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or who could potentially claim? For example, Alex's other parent or another person who also looks after them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's nobody else claiming Child Benefit for Alex. I'm the one looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the necessary requirements:

✓ Alex is 3 years old (under 16)
✓ You are responsible for Alex's care and upbringing
✓ Alex lives with you in your household
✓ You live in the UK and have the right to reside here
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can go ahead and apply for Child Benefit. You should receive payments for Alex until they turn 16 (or until they're 20 if they continue in approved education or training).

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 3 years old, which meets the age requirement of being under 16. The claimant is responsible for Alex, meaning they look after Alex and pay for their upbringing including food, clothes, and housing. Alex lives with the claimant in their household in the UK. The claimant has the right to reside in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 3-year-old child Alex. All key requirements are met: Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant, the claimant is responsible for Alex's care and upbringing, they live in the UK with the right to reside, and no one else is claiming for Alex.
